rulings.Case Title: SEAN NICHOLSON et al VS. HOUSE OF AIR, LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories, Set Two And Req.'S For Documents, Set Two, Req. For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, December 30, 2014, Line 7, DEFENDANT HOUSE OF AIR, LLC Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories, Set Two And Request For Documents, Set Two, Request For Monetary Sanctions. The Request for Monetary Sanctions is granted. rulings.Case Title: SEAN NICHOLSON et al VS. HOUSE OF AIR, LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Further Responses From Defendant To First Set Of Interrogatories And First Set Of Requests For Production Of Documents And Request For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, December 30, 2014, Line 7, PLAINTIFF SEAN NICHOLSON, SHAWN NICHOLSON Motion To Compel Further Responses From Defendant To First Set Of Interrogatories And First Set Of Requests For Production Of Documents And Request For Monetary Sanctions. Plaintiff's Motion to Compel further response from HOA to First Set Request for Production of Document No. 11 is denied as the Court finds the request is overbroad. The Motion is granted in part for Document No. 25. Defendant represents it has not attempted to contact any customers present on December 30, 2012. On the condition it refrains from initiating private contact with such customers in the future, HOA shall send a letter to all customers present at HOA on December 30, 2012, requesting anyone with information about the incident to contact the attorneys for the parties. The parties shall meet and confer on the form of this letter describing the incident, requesting all witnesses, contacting the party attorneys, and providing the attorney contact information. Any party who is contacted by a witness as a result of the letter shall provide the contact information for any such witness to the opposing counsel and the content of any statements made by that witness to counsel. Plaintiff's Motion is denied as to Document No. 38 on the grounds the Court sustains Defendant's overbroad objection. The Motion is denied as to Document No. 39 on the grounds that the Court sustains Defendant's overbroad objection. Plaintiff's request for monetary sanctions is denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: ADOLFO MAGANA VS. CYRIL ALOYSIS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Answers And Form Interrogatories, Special Interrogatories, Demand For Production
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Tuesday, December 30, 2014, Line 2: DEFENDANT CYRIL HACKETT's Motion To Compel Answers And Form Interrogatories, Special Interrogatories, Demand For Production. Pro Tem Judge Peter Van Zandt, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendant CYRIL HACKETT's motion to compel responses to Form Interrogatories (Set One), Special Interrogatories (Set One) and Demand for Production (Set One) is GRANTED. Plaintiff is hereby ORDERED to provide complete, verified responses without objections to Form Interrogatories (Set One), Special Interrogatories (Set One) and Demand for Production (Set One) served by Defendant on July 31, 2014. The responses must be served upon counsel for Defendant CYRIL HACKETT within 15 days of service of Notice of Entry of the Order on this motion. Defendant's request for sanctions is DENIED, without prejudice to seek sanctions in the amount of $650.00 if Plaintiff fails to comply with this Order. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to peter.vanzandt@leclairryan.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/JPT)
rulings.Judge: Judge Pro Tem: Peter Van Zandt

rulings.Case Title: KERI VACA VS. RAFAEL MORALES et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Quash Or In The Alternative Modify Subpoena
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Tuesday, December 30, 2014, Line 3: PLAINTIFF KERI VACA's Motion To Quash Or In The Alternative Modify Subpoena. Pro Tem Judge Peter Van Zandt, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiff KERI VACA's Motion to Quash or Modify Subpoena is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Defendant Rafael Morales is ORDERED to modify the existing subpoenas as follows: The subpoena directed to St. Lukes Women's Center shall be have a timeframe from July 26, 2009 to the present date. The motion is DENIED as to the subpoenas directed to SOMA Orthopedic Medical Group, Health Diagnostics, Mark Dupaix, D.C. and Helaine Shpritz, D.C. as it is understood between the parties that Ms. Vaca had never treated with those healthcare providers before March 6, 2012. The request for sanctions is DENIED. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to peter.vanzandt@leclairryan.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/JPT)
rulings.Judge: Judge Pro Tem: Peter Van Zandt
